chapters.title: The Cell — Basic Unit of Life
key_facts: All living organisms are made of cells
Robert Hooke discovered the cell in 1665
Plant cells have cell wall and chloroplasts; animal cells do not
Mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell
The nucleus controls all cell activities
misconceptions: Cell is visible to naked eye — FALSE
All cells are the same shape — FALSE
chapters.hero_hook: Tiny warriors inside every living thing

chapters.title: Reproduction in Plants
key_facts: Asexual reproduction requires only one parent
Vegetative propagation occurs through roots, stems, and leaves
Pollination is the transfer of pollen from anther to stigma
Seeds are dispersed by wind, water, animals, and explosion
Fertilization produces a zygote
chapters.hero_hook: The secret life cycle of a seed becoming a forest

chapters.title: Adaptation in Plants
key_facts: Xerophytes have thick waxy leaves to reduce water loss
Hydrophytes have air spaces (aerenchyma) for buoyancy
Cactus stores water in its stem
Pitcher plant digests insects to get nitrogen
Mangroves have prop roots and pneumatophores for oxygen in waterlogged soil
misconceptions: All desert plants are cacti — FALSE

chapters.title: Human Body — Digestive System
key_facts: Digestion begins in the mouth with salivary amylase
Stomach produces hydrochloric acid and pepsin
Small intestine is the site of maximum digestion and absorption
Liver produces bile which emulsifies fats
Pancreas produces lipase, amylase, and trypsin
chapters.hero_hook: The incredible journey of a samosa from mouth to energy

chapters.title: Photosynthesis
key_facts: Photosynthesis: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂
Chlorophyll absorbs mainly red and blue light
Photosynthesis occurs in chloroplasts
Light, CO₂, water, and chlorophyll are essential
Oxygen is a byproduct released through stomata

chapters.title: Excretion in Plants
key_facts: Plants excrete oxygen during photosynthesis and CO₂ during respiration
Stomata are pores on leaves that control gas exchange
Resins and tannins are excretory products stored in bark
Transpiration pulls water up from roots (transpiration pull)
chapters.hero_hook: How plants breathe, sweat, and clean themselves

chapters.title: Allergy and Immune System
key_facts: Immunity is the body's ability to resist disease
Antibodies are proteins that neutralize antigens
Allergens trigger histamine release causing allergic reactions
Active immunity is long-lasting; passive immunity is temporary
Vaccination introduces weakened pathogens to build immunity
chapters.hero_hook: Your body's invisible army fighting invaders 24/7

chapters.title: Microorganisms
key_facts: Bacteria are prokaryotic — no membrane-bound nucleus
Viruses are not cells — they are particles of DNA/RNA with protein coat
Lactobacillus converts milk to curd (lactic acid fermentation)
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1929
Pasteurization kills harmful bacteria by heating at 72°C for 15 seconds
Malaria is caused by Plasmodium (protozoa), spread by female Anopheles mosquito
chapters.hero_hook: The invisible world that controls the living world

chapters.title: Cell Division
key_facts: Mitosis produces two identical daughter cells (somatic cells)
Meiosis produces four genetically variable cells (gametes)
Human cells have 46 chromosomes (23 pairs)
DNA replication occurs during S phase of interphase
Cancer is caused by uncontrolled mitotic division
chapters.hero_hook: The miracle of one cell becoming trillions

chapters.title: Conservation of Natural Resources
key_facts: India has 104 national parks and over 550 wildlife sanctuaries
Deforestation leads to soil erosion, loss of biodiversity, climate change
A food chain always starts with a producer (green plant)
Energy transfer in food chain is only 10% at each level (10% law)
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect Bengal tigers
chapters.hero_hook: Earth's last defenders — why every species matters

chapters.title: Basic Biology — Characteristics of Living Things
key_facts: All living things: grow, respire, reproduce, excrete, respond to stimuli
ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) is the energy currency of cells
Aerobic respiration: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + 38 ATP
Anaerobic respiration in yeast: glucose → ethanol + CO₂
MRS GREN: Movement, Respiration, Sensitivity, Growth, Reproduction, Excretion, Nutrition
chapters.hero_hook: What makes you alive? The science of life itself

chapters.title: Flower — Structure and Reproduction
key_facts: Stamen (anther + filament) = male reproductive part
Pistil (stigma + style + ovary) = female reproductive part
Double fertilization is unique to angiosperms
Cross pollination increases genetic variation
After fertilization: ovary → fruit, ovule → seed
chapters.hero_hook: The love story hidden inside every flower

chapters.title: Genetics — Mendel's Laws
key_facts: Gregor Mendel is the Father of Genetics
Law of Segregation: alleles separate during gamete formation
Dominant trait masks recessive in heterozygous condition
Monohybrid cross ratio (F2): 3 dominant : 1 recessive
Punnett square predicts probability of offspring traits
chapters.hero_hook: Why you look like your parents — the code of inheritance

chapters.title: Genetics and Evolution
key_facts: DNA double helix discovered by Watson & Crick in 1953
DNA base pairing: A-T and G-C
Humans have XX (female) and XY (male) sex chromosomes
Mutations are permanent changes in DNA — can be caused by radiation or chemicals
Natural selection: survival of the fittest (Darwin, 1859)
Evolution occurs over millions of years through genetic variation
misconceptions: Humans evolved from monkeys — FALSE (share common ancestor)
chapters.hero_hook: The master code that wrote every living thing on Earth

chapters.title: Human Physiology — Nervous System
key_facts: Neuron is the structural and functional unit of nervous system
Nerve impulse travels electrically along axon, chemically across synapse
Cerebrum controls voluntary actions and intelligence
Cerebellum controls balance and coordination
Reflex arc bypasses the brain for fast responses
chapters.hero_hook: Your body's lightning-fast internet — 120 m/s nerve signals

chapters.title: Human Physiology — Circulatory System
key_facts: Human heart has 4 chambers: 2 atria and 2 ventricles
Double circulation: pulmonary (lungs) and systemic (body)
RBCs carry oxygen via haemoglobin; WBCs fight infection; platelets clot blood
Blood groups: A, B, AB, O — based on antigens on RBC surface
AB is universal recipient; O negative is universal donor
Normal blood pressure: 120/80 mmHg
chapters.hero_hook: The 100,000 heartbeats a day keeping you alive

chapters.title: Human Physiology — Reproductive System
key_facts: Testes produce sperm and testosterone
Ovaries produce eggs and oestrogen/progesterone
Fertilization usually occurs in the fallopian tube
Menstrual cycle is 28 days on average
Placenta supplies nutrients and removes waste from foetus
Puberty in girls: 10-14 years; boys: 12-16 years (approximate)
chapters.hero_hook: The nine-month journey from single cell to a human
